narrative:
Spring begins officially at 10:46 AM EDT and the afternoon is going to feel like it. Sunshine will be filtered a bit by some high cloudiness, but it will be an otherwise very pleasant day. There will be more clouds tonight and maybe a passing light shower late at night, but the theme of this forecast is predominately dry weather for the next several days. Sunday looks like the pick of the weekend with temperatures flirting with daily records.

Spring Has Sprung

Last Updated Fri March 20 at 10:33AM by Scott Stephens

Spring begins officially at 10:46 AM EDT and the afternoon is going to feel like it. Sunshine will be filtered a bit by some high cloudiness, but it will be an otherwise very pleasant day. There will be more clouds tonight and maybe a passing light shower late at night, but the theme of this forecast is predominately dry weather for the next several days. Sunday looks like the pick of the weekend with temperatures flirting with daily records.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

High pressure will be maintaining control of our weather today with sunshine/high clouds and a noticeably milder springtime afternoon.

A weak front brushes the area to our north late tonight and early Saturday, with maybe a passing light shower. Most of the real action will stay north into West Virginia.

Sunshine becomes more prominent again on Sunday, and a surge of warm air from the southern Great Plains will get pushed in our direction. This will boost high temperatures close to record levels for the date.

A cold front moves through on Monday morning with maybe a fleeting light shower. It will send temperatures back down a bit, especially for Tuesday into Wednesday. But really, it will just be back closer to normal for late March.

Moderation kicks back in by late next week ahead of another cold front. That front may cool things down considerably in about a week’s time.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 16, 2026

Quiet weather returns for western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after a hyper-active Monday around the region. Severe weather is followed by a sharp cold shot and mountain snow shower episode, then we moderate nicely for late week through the weekend.

Mar 16, 2026
Mid-March Madness

A strong cold front crosses the Southern Appalachians Monday morning and moves into the Foothills Monday afternoon.

Rain and thunderstorms accompany the frontal passage, and severe thunderstorms are ...

Mar 16, 2026
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.
